requirements.description: Expected core failures use concrete SqlMergerError subclasses.
Parse and I/O errors preserve path, line, operation, context, and cause.
Every ErrorCode has the documented CLI exit-code mapping.
An external boundary logs a failure exactly once and core does not log thrown errors.
ErrorHandler and nested catch-log-rethrow wrappers are removed.
Error refactoring preserves stdout, golden output, and dependency behavior.
SqlMerger uses a normal constructor and typed dependencies without ServiceContainer.
Each command creates and reuses exactly one MergePlan and graph.
Every planning path reports cycles as a typed circular-dependency error.
Core returns data while CLI owns graph and progress presentation.
Merge, info, and validate preserve their observable CLI contracts.
The new pipeline preserves all statement and golden SQL contracts.
Only intentional logger and processor extension points remain public.
The Vite plugin owns a test suite included in recursive workspace tests.
Plugin watching and core planning share recursive discovery.
Silent mode suppresses logs without suppressing generation and uses one logger configuration.
Path containment rejects siblings and generated output cannot self-merge.
Create, update, and delete events each trigger one full regeneration.
Failed regeneration preserves the last good output and reports through Vite.
Vite peer support, tested version, and plugin documentation agree.
Relations use structured schema, name, display, and quoting metadata without changing SQL content.
PostgreSQL identifiers follow quoted and unquoted normalization with a configurable default schema.
Relation keys are collision-safe structured values rather than dotted display strings.
All graph and validation operations use the same relation identity contract.
Relation types collide within one schema and identical names coexist across schemas.
A targeted source lexer preserves quoting, doubled quotes, and quoted dots.
View dependencies cover joins, derived tables, subqueries, and set branches.
CTE aliases are scoped locally while their bodies contribute real dependencies.
View-to-view and view-to-table relation dependencies are deduplicated.
Known SELECT forms are handled explicitly and unknown forms are not silently ignored.
Every advertised dialect has a documented and tested capability matrix.
Core, CLI, plugin, and docs share one supported-dialect registry.
Every supported dialect has P0 parse, ordering, error, and golden fixtures.
Dialect AST differences are isolated behind rules or adapters.

cases.expected: Malformed SQL and processor failures expose typed errors with file, line, processor, and cause context.
Directory, file-read, and output-write failures expose FileSystemError with operation and path.
Every declared error code maps to exit 1, 2, 3, or 4 as documented.
Each failed command emits one sentinel error message, empty stdout, and no nested duplicate logging.
Build, all package tests, and golden output remain green.
The documented constructor and typed dependency seam compile with no container symbols.
Planning builds one graph and merge consumes the returned plan without rebuilding it.
Directory and in-memory planning report CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY for cyclic input.
Programmatic planning returns diagnostics without graph headers or progress output.
Merge, info, and validate preserve SQL stdout, stderr presentation, diagnostics, and exit codes.
Every existing correct scenario produces the same normalized SQL through MergePlan.
pnpm -r test executes and passes the Vite plugin suite.
Nested SQL files are watched, planned, and emitted in dependency order.
Silent buildStart writes the same output as info mode while producing no SQLsmith log entries.
Sibling paths and generated output are excluded while true descendants are accepted.
Create, update, and delete events each perform one rediscovery and regeneration.
A malformed update leaves the last good output unchanged and build failures reach Vite once.
Peer range, tested Vite major, package metadata, and README support statement agree.
Parsed relations expose immutable structured identifiers while merged statement SQL stays lossless.
PostgreSQL unquoted and quoted names resolve with configured default-schema semantics.
Quoted dots and schema separators produce distinct collision-safe relation keys.
Duplicate, missing, self, source-order, sort, and diagnostics agree on relation identity.
Same-schema relation types conflict and same display names in different schemas coexist.
The source lexer distinguishes quoting, doubled quotes, and dots inside identifiers.
Views depend on relations found in joins, derived tables, subqueries, and every set branch.
CTE bodies contribute dependencies while local and recursive aliases never become missing relations.
View-to-view and view-to-table chains sort correctly and repeated references are deduplicated.
Unsupported SELECT AST forms produce an explicit diagnostic or typed error instead of silent omission.
Every advertised dialect row has executable cases for each claimed P0 capability.
Core type, CLI choices, plugin type, and documentation resolve from one supported registry.
Every remaining supported dialect passes base, FK, duplicate, missing, view, order, and golden cases.
Dialect-specific AST inputs produce the common relation model through isolated adapters.

traces.pendingReason: The plugin currently has no test script.
The Vite contract suite is created in stage 5.
Silent currently returns before generation.
Containment and output exclusion tests are added in stage 5.
Create, update, and delete are unified behind watchChange in stage 5.
Atomic output and last-good behavior do not exist yet.
Vite peer and development majors are currently inconsistent.
Structured relation identifiers are introduced in stage 6a.
PostgreSQL normalization fixtures are added in stage 6a.
Collision-safe keys do not exist yet.
Graph identity fixtures are added in stage 6a.
Cross-schema and shared relation-namespace cases are added in stage 6a.
The targeted identifier lexer is implemented in stage 6a.
Nested SELECT dependency traversal is implemented in stage 6b.
Scoped CTE traversal is implemented in stage 6b.
View relation-chain fixtures are added in stage 6b.
The unknown-AST policy is implemented and tested in stage 6b.
The executable dialect capability matrix is added in stage 6c.
A shared supported-dialect registry is introduced in stage 6c.
Capability-gated dialect fixtures are completed in stage 6c.
Dialect adapters and their common-model contract are added in stage 6c.
